sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the upper right leg following a bowling injury, characterized as tenderness to palpation and pain with weight-bearing. He reports mild bruising on the back of his right leg. He has a history of atopic eczema treated with fluocinonide. He previously underwent a colectomy for diverticulitis.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical examination reveals tenderness to the lateral aspect of the right upper leg. X-ray demonstrates no fracture or significant swelling. Vital signs are within normal limits.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient likely has a contusion of the right upper leg. The history of atopic eczema is relevant.
medical_concepts: contusion of the right upper leg

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Start mobic 15 milligrams once daily. Recommend ice packs for 20 minutes every 2-3 hours. Follow up in one week if symptoms do not improve. Dragon, please order the prescribed medications and procedures as described.

medical_concepts: contusion of the right upper leg
